name: Vía Verde Val del Zafán
description:
The railway project that was to link Puebla de Híjar, in Teruel, with Tortosa, in Tarragona, was never completed. The line operated partially for a few decades, until the tracks were finally raised in 1973. In the following years, the route was conditioned, turning it into the current greenway that connects Alcañiz with Tortosa. This Route is perfect to start cycling with the family. Without a doubt, it is an ambitious proposal, since there are 102 kilometers to travel. But due to its characteristics, when pedaling on a road segregated from traffic that goes downhill for most of the route and that has a good offer of tourist services, it constitutes a very good proposal to start cycling trips with the family.

The Route can be divided into several days, depending on the kilometers that you want to do per day and the accommodation offer. To give you an idea, if you wanted to do it in five days, these would be the possible stages:

Alcañiz - Valljunquera (21 kilometres)
Valljunquera - Arnes (28 kilometres)
Arnes-Bot (17 kilometers)
Bot - Benifallet (20 kilometres)
Benifallet - Tortosa (24 kilometres)

After the first kilometres, little by little you approach the natural barrier of the Natural Park of Los Puertos. This large mountainous massif separates Teruel from Castellón and Tarragona and, as it passes through the towns of Arnes, Horta de Sant Joan or Bot, offers you a magnificent backdrop for the route, with its imposing rock formations, such as the Rocas de Benet or the Penyagalera. You can visit the historic center of Arnes, declared a site of cultural interest, or Horta de Sant Joan. In this peculiar town, perched on a promontory, you can visit the Els Ports Ecomuseoi or the Picasso Center, as well as its characteristic old town with concentric streets.

In Bot, the Route turns southeast again to find the course of the Ebro River and head towards Tortosa. 10 kilometers after Bot, next to a bar on the same greenway, you can take a detour to visit Pinell de Brai, six kilometers away by road. In Bot you can visit the Renaissance church of Sant Blai. In Pinell de Brai, in addition to finding accommodation and restaurants, you can visit the modernist-style cooperative winery, designed and built by César Martinell, a disciple of Gaudí.

Benifallet or Xerta are two of the riverside towns that you find when you reach the Ebro and that can serve as the end of the stage. In the case of Benifallet, when you arrive at the old train station on the greenway, you can take a road that takes you to the town, located five kilometers away. If you decide to go to Xerta, the greenway crosses it and you should not take any detours. In Xerta you can visit its dam (assut), an engineering work from the 15th century, located in the middle of the Ebro river. The last kilometers to Tortosa are very flat and run between crops, not far from the course of the Ebro. In Tortosa, We recommend visiting the Royal Colleges, the most important Renaissance monument in Catalonia, the spectacular castle of La Suda, from the 10th century, where the only open-air Islamic necropolis in Catalonia is preserved, or the Cathedral of Santa Maria.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alcañiz traverse a varied landscape in the province of Teruel, Spain. The region features the meanders of the Guadalope River, the serene artificial lake of La Estanca de Alcañiz, and extensive pine forests. Routes often follow the generally flat gradients of the Vía Verde Val de Zafán, which includes numerous tunnels and viaducts, or venture into more undulating terrain towards the Matarraña region and the Ports mountain range. Best touring cycling routes around Alcañiz The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde Val del Zafán, a difficult 63.1 miles (101.6 km) trail that takes about 7 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route follows a disused railway line, passing through numerous tunnels and viaducts. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valdealgorfa – View of Valdealgorfa loop from Alcañiz, a moderate 19.6 miles (31.6 km) path. This route offers views of the surrounding countryside and the town of Valdealgorfa. Local touring cyclists also love the Alcañiz – Castle of the Calatravos loop from Alcañiz, a 27.1 miles (43.6 km) trail leading past the historic Castle of the Calatravos, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcañiz is defined by the Vía Verde Val de Zafán, the Guadalope River, and pine forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Alcañiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 7 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcañiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Alcañiz?
A: Touring cycling routes in Alcañiz offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll find generally flat gradients along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220146249/via-verde-val-del-zafan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Val del Zafán&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a disused railway line. Other routes feature a mix of paved surfaces, unpaved rural paths, and mountain tracks. Expect varied scenery, from the meanders of the Guadalope River and cultivated fields to extensive pine forests and more undulating terrain towards the Matarraña region and the Ports mountain range.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Alcañiz?
A: Yes, Alcañiz offers several easy touring cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220146249/via-verde-val-del-zafan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Val del Zafán&lt;/a&gt;, while long, is known for its generally flat gradient (max 3%), making it accessible for a wide range of cyclists, including families. Overall, there are 4 easy routes available, providing gentle options for those new to touring cycling or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes around Alcañiz offer a blend of historical and natural sights. You can cycle past the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Calatravos&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient fortress. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220146249/via-verde-val-del-zafan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Val del Zafán&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, featuring numerous tunnels and spectacular viaducts. Other routes may lead you to the serene artificial lake of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Estanca de Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt;, or past unique natural elements like the "Mangranera" Aleppo pine forest. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5226851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock Art Paintings Near the Guadalope River&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO Heritage Site a few kilometers from Alcañiz.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Alcañiz?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31487879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdealgorfa – View of Valdealgorfa loop from Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views of the surrounding countryside. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz – Castle of the Calatravos loop from Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past the historic castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz – Estanca de Alcañiz loop from Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt; provides a pleasant ride around the lake.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcañiz?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and generally stable weather conditions. While summer can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons can still be enjoyable. Winter cycling is possible, but it's advisable to check weather conditions as temperatures can drop, especially in the more mountainous areas.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in Alcañiz, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are 34 touring cycling routes around Alcañiz. These routes cater to various ability levels: 4 are classified as easy, 17 as moderate, and 13 as difficult. This variety ensures options for beginners, intermediate riders, and experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcañiz?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alcañiz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the flat Vía Verde to the more challenging mountain terrain, and the opportunity to combine rides with visits to historical sites like the Castle of the Calatravos.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options in the Alcañiz area?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220146249/via-verde-val-del-zafan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Val del Zafán&lt;/a&gt; is a significant long-distance option, spanning approximately 100 to 126 kilometers. This route, following a disused railway line, offers a unique journey through diverse landscapes, including numerous tunnels and viaducts, making it suitable for multi-day touring or a challenging day trip.

Q: Can I find bike rental services in Alcañiz?
A: Yes, support services like bike rental are available in the Alcañiz area, particularly for those looking to explore the popular Vía Verde. It's recommended to check with local tourism offices or specialized bike shops in Alcañiz for current rental options and availability.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or rivers?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Alcañiz feature water elements. You can find routes that traverse the meanders of the Guadalope River, offering picturesque riverside cycling. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz – Estanca de Alcañiz loop from Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt; takes you around the serene artificial lake of La Estanca de Alcañiz, providing beautiful views and a tranquil riding experience.

Q: What kind of natural monuments can be explored while cycling?
A: Beyond the general landscapes, you can explore specific natural monuments. For instance, some routes might lead you near the unique ecosystem of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5818573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Saladas&lt;/a&gt;, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5818576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruppia maritima and other species of the Saladas de Chiprana&lt;/a&gt;. You could also encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5383041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Holm Oaks of La Sarda&lt;/a&gt;, which are recognized natural features in the region.
tips: The station is in a spectacular location and relatively well preserved. Very easy access from the N-420 at the junction to go to Valljunquera. Ideal for walking along the greenway or for cycling along the route.

It is believed that the origins of Arnes lie in a Saracen farmhouse that was later conquered by Alfonso el Casto in the 14th century. It is located at the gates of the Els Ports massif, very close to the Algars river.

Thanks to its privileged location and the influence of the greenway that passes next to the town, Arnes has developed a good range of rural tourism. In addition to the landscapes and nature excursions, Arnes has a beautiful historic center and a renowned building: the town hall building, one of the most important civil constructions of the Catalan Renaissance, built by Joan Vilabona de Queretes. The remains of the apse of the old Gothic church next to the current 18th century church have also been recovered. In 1992 the nucleus of Arnes was declared a Historic Site by the Generalitat de Catalunya.

The Val de Zafán greenway passes very close to the town of Horta de Sant Joan, located on a promontory.

It is worth taking a detour and going up to the town, and walking through its narrow concentric streets that surround the disappeared castle. Its historic center houses interesting samples of Gothic architecture, such as the church of Sant Joan Baptista, Renaissance buildings such as the town hall, Casa Clúa, Casa Pitarch or Casa del Delme).

The viaduct known as Pont de l'Arc is located between Bot and Horta de Sant Joan. It is the second largest built in stone in Spain and is curved and peralted and is built from a succession of small superimposed arches.

The Canaleta river is a tributary of the Ebro that originates in the Espina mountain range, north of the Els Ports massif. Between Horta de Sant Joan and Bot the river has eroded the rocks forming pools called "Olles de Bot".

Old railway station in the municipality of Bot, in Terra Alta, which belonged to the Val de Zafán railway. The railway complex that connected La Puebla de Híjar, in the province of Teruel, with Tortosa, in Tarragona, has been replaced by a greenway. In the old Bot station there is currently a bar where you can stop during the route.
This tunnel connects Terra Alta with Tarragona in the Natural Park of Los Puertos.

The Sanctuary of the Mare de Déu de la Fontcalda dates from the 14th century, although the current building is from 1753. It is neoclassical in style, with a single nave with a barrel vault and a small transept with an octagonal dome.

This place has been known since the 14th century, where an image of the Mare de Déu de la Fontcalda was venerated. La Font Calda gets its name from the hot springs that gush out at the foot of the sanctuary. The water comes out at 25º and in addition to the sanctuary, buildings were built to house people who came to become priests or simply to spend the weekend or vacation.
At a lofty height, an old railway viaduct leads right through the gorge - and on the other side the Vía Verde disappears into a tunnel again.
Xerta is a perfect town to make a stop to visit and to regain strength in one of the bars in the main square in the center.

This is the initial stretch of the Val de Zafán greenway, leaving Tortosa towards Alcañiz. It occupies the layout of the old railway line between Puebla de Híjar and Tortosa, dismantled in the 1970s. Today it is a first-class tourist attraction, thanks to being a segregated cycle path and enjoying numerous tourist and scenic attractions along its route.
